Chip rendering is fully data-driven from the JSON — _renderChips() / _renderGroupedChips() write DOM from presets entries. No per-chip HTML in index.html except grouping wrappers.
Character info strip auto-fills portrait, name, hair/eye/age/species/body traits from char override on every open.
Studio scene state (_studioScene) is a superset of _scene — includes clothing top/bottom/footwear as separate fields. buildImagePrompt receives a merged view.
Gallery strip inside studio shows last 20 char images; click-to-load lets the user select an existing image as the current preview.
S key opens the studio. Existing quick modal stays on the toolbar image-plus icon.
_studioWired flag prevents double-wiring of nav and quick-capture event listeners on repeated opens.
Activity section uses grouped sub-headers (Solo/Oral/Penetrative/Finishing/BDSM) — rendered via _renderGroupedChips with per-group labels, matching the herahaven.ai UX pattern.
rules_reinforced: All chip populations are data-driven — no hardcoded chip HTML for studio. Presets JSON is the single source of truth for chip values.
Studio uses opacity transition (not display toggle) for open/close — consistent with scene-codex pattern.
Every new overlay must wire close on Escape via existing keydown handler or its own listener — studio closes on X click only currently (intentional: prevent accidental close).

for_new_llm.read_this_first: You are about to work on trap.lol. The file you are reading is status/logs/logs.json at the root of the trap.lol repository. It is the site's persistent memory and single source of truth. Read it in full before touching any code. The _protocol section is your operating instructions — it tells you exactly what to update and when. The rules and anti_patterns sections are load-bearing — violating them will produce work that gets reverted. Your last act in every session is to update this file (status/logs/logs.json) and commit it.
the_five_laws: 1. No frameworks. No build steps. Vanilla only.
2. All data lives in JSON. All HTML is scaffolding.
3. esc() on all JSON data going into innerHTML. DOMPurify on markdown.
4. lucide.createIcons() only after all HTML is injected.
5. The aesthetic is Dune × Arcane. Dark, glowing, precise, poetic. Never corporate, never default.
for_new_llm.the_sixth_law: 6. Update this file (logs.json) at the end of every session. Prepend to commits[]. Prepend to sessions[]. Update _meta.last_updated. This is not optional.
how_to_help_effectively: When asked to add a new section or item: edit the relevant JSON file. Do not touch the HTML.
When asked to add a new page: follow the pattern of the nearest existing page. Match its CSS variable usage, its glass morphism, its mono typography.
When asked to fix a bug: read the file top to bottom first. The architecture is intentional. The bug is almost never where you think it is.
When asked to improve performance: look for DOM thrashing, unnecessary re-renders, unscoped lucide.createIcons() calls, unscoped IntersectionObserver selectors.
When asked to improve UI: the answer is almost always 'add a glow' or 'add a sweep' or 'add an entrance animation'. The palette is fixed. Don't introduce new colours.
When unsure about tone for UI copy: terse. mono. ALL CAPS for states. lowercase for whispers. Never full sentences in labels.
At session end: update logs.json per _protocol before committing.
what_trap_values_most: Sovereignty — full control, no external dependencies beyond what's necessary
Precision — every pixel intentional, every byte justified
Aesthetic consistency — the palette and feel must be coherent across all pages
Speed of iteration — ship and refine, don't over-architect
Security at the boundary — validate user input, sanitize rendered content, expose nothing accidentally
Surprise — the gacha pull, the terminal shortcut system, the cursor orb — the site rewards exploration
Memory — the logs system exists because trap doesn't want to re-explain the same context every session. Honour it.

pending_work._note: This section tracks features requested by trap that are partially or fully unimplemented. An LLM reading this file must check this section before asking what to do. Status values: 'not_started' | 'in_progress' | 'done' | 'deferred' | 'wont_do'. Update status and notes after each session. Never remove entries — only update them.

system_audit.description: A comprehensive point-in-time analysis of the project's architecture, intents, strengths, and vulnerabilities.

directory_intents.glass/: The structural heart of the main hub. Contains CSS modules, vanilla JS logic, and the flat JSON files that drive the hub's UI (apps, tools, bots, hosting). It is intended to be completely static and declarative.
directory_intents.playground/characters/: The Underdark. A highly complex, isolated sub-app for LLM roleplay. Intended to handle dynamic character loading (V2 spec), sliding context windows, image/video generation, and local persistence. It is the most actively developed and volatile directory.
directory_intents.art/: ECHOES gallery. Intended for immersive, high-aesthetic artwork viewing with a hidden gacha pull mechanic. Self-contained styling and logic.
directory_intents.status/: Uplink Sentinel and the Logs terminal. Intended to probe infrastructure health and host the site's self-referential memory (logs.json).
directory_intents.guide/: Markdown reader for documentation. Intended to securely parse and render .md files with DOMPurify.
directory_intents.radar/: Torrent and TorBox integration. Intended to interface securely with external debrid APIs.

what_it_does_well: Speed & Simplicity of Deployment: The zero-build, pure vanilla architecture means changes are instantly live. No NPM install, no Webpack compilation.
Sovereign Integrity: Hard reliance on local storage and direct API routing (nano-gpt) keeps user data off intermediary servers.
Aesthetic Cohesion: The Dune x Arcane styling is flawlessly maintained across diverse sub-apps via strict CSS variable usage.
Extensibility: Adding new content (bots, tools, characters) is entirely data-driven via JSON, requiring zero HTML/JS changes.
AI Modularity: The prompt engine in The Underdark seamlessly weaves sliding history, physical anchors, and recursive summaries.
known_bugs_and_weaknesses: Vanilla DOM Desync: Lacking a framework like React/Vue, complex state changes (especially in The Underdark's Scene Codex and Image Studio) rely on manual DOM manipulation. This risks UI desyncs if elements are updated out of order.

Storage Quotas: Heavy reliance on localStorage for chat history, character states, and logs API keys. Prolonged RP sessions with multiple characters could hit the 5MB browser limit, requiring a shift to IndexedDB.

Lucide Icon Fragility: The strict rule to call lucide.createIcons() only after HTML injection works for static pages, but dynamic sub-apps require careful scoped reinjection ({ nodes: [...] }) to avoid massive performance hits or missing icons.

Mobile Layout Density: As The Underdark gains god-view panels, video polling, and image studio modals, the mobile UI is becoming increasingly crowded and difficult to navigate without overlapping absolute positioning.
Video Polling Inaccuracy: Nano-GPT video generation lacks precise progress percentages, forcing the UI to rely on heuristic time-elapsed estimation.
